description: Migration adds lifecycle columns/state to MSP SSO domain persistence schema.
Migration creates optional verification challenge persistence for EE ownership checks.
Migration rollback removes lifecycle/challenge schema changes cleanly.
Backfill marks existing EE active domain rows with verified-compatible legacy status.
Backfill marks existing CE active domain rows with advisory status.
Domain normalization helper trims, lowercases, and strips unsupported decorations.
Domain validation rejects malformed values with deterministic neutral errors.
EE list-claims action denies client users and unauthorized internal users.
EE list-claims action returns normalized domains with lifecycle metadata.
EE request-claim action creates pending claim and challenge material for a new domain.
EE request-claim action is idempotent for an existing pending claim by same tenant.
EE challenge refresh action rotates challenge token material and invalidates previous challenge.
EE verify action with valid DNS challenge promotes pending claim to verified.
EE verify action with invalid/missing DNS challenge remains pending and returns neutral admin error.
EE revoke action transitions verified claim to revoked and removes takeover eligibility.
EE conflict policy blocks second tenant from becoming verified owner of the same domain.
CE advisory add-domain action persists active advisory registration.
CE advisory remove-domain action deactivates advisory registration.
EE settings UI renders domain claim lifecycle table with status badges.
EE settings UI renders verification instructions for pending claims.
EE settings UI shows neutral actionable error when verification fails.
CE settings UI renders advisory registration copy and guidance.
CE settings UI add/remove controls persist advisory registrations successfully.
Settings copy explicitly states unmanaged domains use Nine Minds app-level fallback.
Discovery helper evaluates edition and claim lifecycle before selecting tenant/app source.
EE discovery with verified claim and tenant Google credentials returns tenant source + google provider.
EE discovery with verified claim and tenant Microsoft credentials returns tenant source + azure-ad provider.
EE discovery with pending claim returns app-level fallback providers only.
EE discovery with revoked claim returns app-level fallback providers only.
EE discovery with ambiguous domain ownership returns app-level fallback providers only.
CE discovery with advisory registered domain can return tenant-scoped provider eligibility.
CE discovery with unregistered domain returns app-level fallback providers.
Unresolved domain in both editions returns app-level fallback provider set.
Discover endpoint invalid-email path returns invariant neutral schema.
Discover endpoint rate-limit path returns same neutral schema and behavior.
Discover endpoint logging excludes raw email and keeps only safe metadata.
Resolver in EE with verified claim context selects tenant credential source.
Resolver in EE with non-verified claim context uses app fallback or generic failure per eligibility.
Resolver denies provider attempts outside discovered allow-list with generic response.
Resolver stale discovery context is revalidated and cannot force unauthorized tenant source.
Resolver invalid payload, rate-limit, and source-failure responses remain externally indistinguishable.
Discovery cookie payload remains signed, short-lived, and free of provider secrets.
Resolution cookie payload remains signed, short-lived, and free of provider secrets.
MSP credentials login succeeds unchanged when domain claim states vary.
Client portal signin flow remains unchanged and does not call MSP discovery endpoints.
CE build wiring resolves MSP SSO entry to discovery-enabled provider buttons implementation.
MSP login form passes normalized email prop into SSO discovery component in both editions.
SSO buttons remain disabled for invalid email and while discovery is in flight.
SSO buttons enable only providers returned by discovery response and keep unsupported buttons disabled.
Disabled SSO button clicks never trigger resolver/start request.
Remembered provider preference is only applied when provider remains eligible after discovery.
Docs describe EE request-verify-revoke lifecycle and DNS ownership verification steps.
Docs describe CE advisory registration behavior and non-blocking ownership model.
Docs describe Nine Minds fallback provider prerequisites and unmanaged-domain behavior.
DB-backed integration happy path: EE verified claim + tenant Microsoft credentials returns tenant source and ["azure-ad"].
DB-backed integration guard: EE pending claim with tenant credentials still returns app fallback source.
DB-backed integration guard: second EE tenant cannot verify takeover for already-verified domain.
DB-backed integration guard: revoked EE claim no longer enables tenant takeover routing.
DB-backed integration CE advisory path: registered advisory domain can route to tenant source when tenant credentials exist.
Route contract preserves `/auth/msp/signin` entry path and callbackUrl passthrough under new lifecycle rules.
